# Figure description: скв_396.jpg

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (геологический разрез скважины)** — specifically a composite well log or lithological-stratigraphic column with accompanying geophysical curves. It appears to be a multi-track well log format typical of Soviet-era exploration drilling documentation, displaying lithological description alongside one or more geophysical logging curves (likely gamma-ray, resistivity, or spontaneous potential logs) plotted against depth.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a single borehole or well, likely referenced in the context of the regional survey of northern Kazakhstan (map sheets M-42 and/or N-42). The title at the top appears to read **"РОК ТОСТЫ-ТЛАБМ"** or similar (partially legible Cyrillic text), which may be a transliteration of a local geographic name or borehole designation. The log appears to cover a substantial depth range — visually the log spans what appears to be several hundred metres based on the length of the track, though the precise depth values are difficult to read at this resolution. The header contains well identification information, survey metadata, and possibly coordinates or sheet references.

---

## Key Content

The figure is structured in the standard Soviet composite well-log format with the following observable tracks (left to right):

1. **Left margin columns**: Contain lithological and stratigraphic information — likely including:
   - Depth scale (in metres)
   - Core recovery or interval notation
   - Stratigraphic horizon/formation assignments (свита/горизонт)
   - Lithological symbols (hatching patterns visible, suggesting alternating rock types such as sandstone, clay/shale, carbonate, or crystalline basement)

2. **Central geophysical curve tracks**: Two prominent wiggle/curve traces are visible running the full length of the log. These show:
   - Significant amplitude variations throughout
   - Several pronounced **anomalous deflections** (large peaks/troughs) particularly in the upper and middle portions of the log
   - A notable **high-amplitude excursion** in the upper third of the log, possibly indicating a resistive or radioactive interval
   - The curves appear to be **mirror-image or complementary pairs**, consistent with **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** and **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / spontaneous potential)** logging curves, or possibly **gamma-ray (ГК)** paired with another parameter
   - Mid-section shows relatively **subdued, low-amplitude** variations suggesting more homogeneous lithology (possibly a thick clay or basement section)
   - Lower portion shows renewed moderate variability

3. **Right margin columns**: Mirror the left side, containing additional formation or interval data, possibly including:
   - Porosity estimates
   - Fluid or mineral notations
   - Correlation markers

4. **A notable annotation** approximately one-third down the log reads **"ТОЛЩАГЕТЕН РЕТ"** or similar — possibly a formation name or stratigraphic marker horizon.

5. **Another annotation** mid-log appears to read **"КОНТРОЛЬНОЕ КОЛОННА"** (control column) or similar, suggesting a reference or calibration section.

6. The lithological column on the left shows **distinct hatching patterns** changing at various depths, indicating multiple lithological units. Crosshatching typical of crystalline or metamorphic basement is visible in the lower portion, while more regular horizontal or diagonal hatching in the upper portions suggests sedimentary cover sequences.

## Text and Labels

Legible and partially legible text (transcribed as closely as possible given image resolution):

**Header (top):**
- **"РОК ТОСТЫ-ТЛАБМ"** (title — possibly well/location name)
- Smaller text below likely includes: well number, location coordinates, drilling dates, depth information, and responsible geologist — standard Soviet report header fields

- **"ТОЛЩАГЕТЕН РЕТ"** or **"ТАРАГЕТЕН РЕТ"** — possibly **"ТАРАGETЕН РЕТ"**, likely a formation or stratigraphic unit name
- **"РАЗРЕЗ II"** or **"РАЗРЕЗ III"** — "Section II/III", indicating stratigraphic subdivision

**Lower section:**
- **"КОНТРОЛЬНОЕ КОЛОННА"** — "Control column" — a calibration or reference annotation

**Right-hand margin:**
- Column headers likely reading **"ГЛУБИНА"** (depth), **"МОЩНОСТЬ"** (thickness), **"ОПИСАНИЕ ПОРОД"** (rock description)

**Scale bar / axis:**
- Depth values are present along the left edge but not resolvable at this resolution; the total log length suggests a depth range possibly on the order of **500–1500 m**

## Research Value

This composite well log provides a direct record of the **subsurface lithological and geophysical character** of the drilled section at a specific location within the survey area, allowing correlation with regional seismic and gravity/magnetic data collected during the 1983–1984 survey. The geophysical curves (likely resistivity and SP or gamma-ray) enable identification of **formation boundaries, reservoir intervals, and potentially mineralised horizons** — particularly the anomalous high-amplitude sections in the upper and middle log which may indicate hydrocarbon-bearing, evaporitic, or ore-bearing intervals. For a research assistant reconstructing the mineral potential assessment of this part of northern Kazakhstan, this log is a key calibration dataset that ties surface geophysical anomalies to actual rock types and depths encountered in the borehole.
